sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: ὅτι τὸ βούλεσθαί μʼ ἔκνιζε · σὸς δὲ δοῦλος οὐκ ἔφυν .
sections.translation: My wish to do it gave the spur , for I am no slave to you .

sections.label: Section 331
sections.original: οὐχὶ δεινά ; τὸν ἐμὸν οἰκεῖν οἶκον οὐκ ἐάσομαι ;
sections.translation: Infamous ! Am I not to be allowed the management of my own house ?

sections.label: Section 332
sections.original: πλάγια γὰρ φρονεῖς , τὰ μὲν νῦν , τὰ δὲ πάλαι , τὰ δʼ αὐτίκα .
sections.translation: No , for you think crooked thoughts , one thing now , another formerly , and something different presently .

sections.label: Section 333
sections.original: εὖ κεκόμψευσαι πονηρά · γλῶσσʼ ἐπίφθονον σοφή .
sections.translation: Most exquisite refining on evil themes ! [*] A hateful thing the tongue of cleverness !

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: νοῦς δέ γʼ οὐ βέβαιος ἄδικον κτῆμα κοὐ σαφὲς φίλοις .
sections.translation: Yes , but a mind unstable is an unjust possession , disloyal to friends .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: βούλομαι δέ σʼ ἐξελέγξαι , καὶ σὺ μήτʼ ὀργῆς ὕπο
sections.translation: Now I am anxious to test you , and do not seek from rage to turn aside from the truth , nor will I on my part overstrain [*] the case . Do you remember when you were all eagerness to captain the Danaids against Troy , making a pretence of declining , though eager for it in your heart ; how humble you were then , taking each man by the hand

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: καὶ θύρας ἔχων ἀκλῄστους τῷ θέλοντι δημοτῶν
sections.translation: and keeping open doors for every fellow-townsman who cared to enter , affording each in turn chance to speak with you , even though some did not wish it , seeking by these methods to purchase popularity from all bidders ? Then when you had secured the command , there came a change over your manners ; you were no longer so cordial as before to former friends ,

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: δυσπρόσιτος ἔσω τε κλῄθρων σπάνιος . ἄνδρα δʼ οὐ χρεὼν
sections.translation: but hard of access , seldom to be found at home . But the man of real worth ought not to change his manners in the hour of prosperity , but should then show himself most staunch to friends , when his own good fortune can help them most effectually .

sections.label: Section 349
sections.original: ταῦτα μέν σε πρῶτʼ ἐπῆλθον , ἵνα σε πρῶθʼ ηὗρον κακόν .
sections.translation: This was the first cause I had to reprove you , for it was here I first discovered your villainy ;

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ὡς δʼ ἐς Αὖλιν ἦλθες αὖθις χὡ Πανελλήνων στρατός ,
sections.translation: but afterwards , when you came [*] to Aulis with all the gathered hosts of Hellas , you were of no account ; no ! the want of a favorable breeze filled you with consternation at the chance dealt out by the gods . [*] Then the Danaids began demanding that you should send the fleet away instead of vainly toiling on at Aulis ; what dismay and confusion was then depicted in your looks , to think that you , with a thousand

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: χιλίων ἄρχων τὸ Πριάμου πεδίον ἐμπλήσεις δορός .
sections.translation: ships at your command , had not occupied the plains of Priam with your armies ! And you would ask my counsel , What am I to do ? What scheme can I devise , where find one ? — to save yourself being stripped of your command and losing your fair fame . Next when Calchas bade you offer your daughter in sacrifice to Artemis , declaring that the Danaids should then sail , you were overjoyed , [*]

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: ἄσμενος θύσειν ὑπέστης παῖδα · καὶ πέμπεις ἑκών ,
sections.translation: and gladly undertook to offer the girl , and of your own accord — never allege compulsion — you are sending word to your wife to despatch your daughter here on pretence of wedding Achilles . And after all you turn round and have been caught casting your letter to this effect : I will no longer be my daughter ’ s murderer . Exactly so ! [*]

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: οὗτος αὑτός ἐστιν αἰθὴρ ὃς τάδʼ ἤκουσεν σέθεν .
sections.translation: This is the same air that heard you say it . [*] Countless others have done the same ; they make an effort while in power , and then retire dishonorably , sometimes owing to the senselessness of the citizens , sometimes deservedly , because they are too feeble of themselves to maintain their watch upon the state .

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: Ἑλλάδος μάλιστʼ ἔγωγε τῆς ταλαιπώρου στένω ,
sections.translation: For my part , I am more sorry for our unhappy Hellas , whose purpose was to read these worthless foreigners a lesson , while now she will let them escape and mock her , thanks to you and your daughter . May I never appoint a man to rule my country or lead its warriors because of his ! [*] Sense is what the general must have ;

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: πόλεος ὡς ἄρχων ἀνὴρ πᾶς , ξύνεσιν ἢν ἔχων τύχῃ .
sections.translation: since any man , with ordinary intelligence , can govern a state . [*]

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: δεινὸν κασιγνήτοισι γίγνεσθαι λόγους
sections.translation: For brothers to come to words and blows , whenever they disagree , is terrible .

sections.label: Section 378
sections.original: βούλομαί σʼ εἰπεῖν κακῶς εὖ , βραχέα , μὴ λίαν ἄνω
sections.translation: I wish to rebuke you in turn , [*] briefly , not lifting my eyes too high shamelessly , but in more sober fashion ,

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ὡς ἀδελφὸν ὄντʼ · ἀνὴρ γὰρ χρηστὸς αἰδεῖσθαι φιλεῖ .
sections.translation: as a brother ; for it is a good man ’ s way to be considerate . Tell me , why this burst of fury , these bloodshot eyes ? who wrongs you ? what is it you want ? are you longing to have a virtuous wife ? WeIl , I cannot supply you ; for she , whom you once had , was ill-controlled by you . Am I then , a man who never went astray , to suffer for your sins ?

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: οὐ δάκνει σε τὸ φιλότιμον τοὐμόν , ἀλλʼ ἐν ἀγκάλαις
sections.translation: or is it my popularity that galls you ? No ! it is the longing you have to keep a beautiful woman in your embrace , casting reason and honor to the winds . A bad man ’ s pleasures are like himself . Am I mad , if I change to wiser counsels , after previously deciding amiss ? Yours is the madness rather in wishing to recover

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἀναλαβεῖν θέλεις , θεοῦ σοι τὴν τύχην διδόντος εὖ .
sections.translation: a wicked wife , once you had lost her — a stroke of heaven-sent luck . Those foolish suitors swore that oath to Tyndareus in their longing to wed ; but Hope was the goddess that led them on , [*] I think , and she it was that brought it about rather than you and your strength . So take the field with them ; they are ready for it [*] in the folly of their hearts ; for the deity is not without insight , but is able to discern

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: τοὺς κακῶς παγέντας ὅρκους καὶ κατηναγκασμένους .
sections.translation: where oaths have been wrongly pledged or forcibly extorted . I will not slay my children , nor [*] shall your interests be prospered by justice in your vengeance for a worthless wife , while I am left wasting , night and day , in sorrow for what I did to one of my own flesh and blood , contrary to all law and justice .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: ταῦτά σοι βραχέα λέλεκται καὶ σαφῆ καὶ ῥᾴδια ·
sections.translation: Here is your answer shortly given , clear and easy to understand ; and if you will not come to your senses , I shall do the best for myself .

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: οἵδʼ αὖ διάφοροι τῶν πάρος λελεγμένων
sections.translation: This differs from your previous declaration , but there is good in it , your child ’ s reprieve .

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: αἰαῖ , φίλους ἄρʼ οὐχὶ κεκτήμην τάλας .
sections.translation: Ah me , how sad my lot ! I have no friends then after all .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: εἰ τοὺς φίλους γε μὴ θέλεις ἀπολλύναι .
sections.translation: Friends you have , if you do not seek their destruction .

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: δείξεις δὲ ποῦ μοι πατρὸς ἐκ ταὐτοῦ γεγώς ;
sections.translation: Where will you find any proof that you are sprung from the same father as I ?

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: συνσωφρονεῖν σοι βούλομʼ , ἀλλʼ οὐ συννοσεῖν .
sections.translation: It is your moderation , not your madness , that I share by nature .

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: ἐς κοινὸν ἀλγεῖν τοῖς φίλοισι χρὴ φίλους .
sections.translation: Friends should sympathize with friends in sorrow .

sections.label: Section 409
sections.original: εὖ δρῶν παρακάλει μʼ , ἀλλὰ μὴ λυπῶν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Claim my help by kindly service , not by paining me .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: οὐκ ἄρα δοκεῖ σοι τάδε πονεῖν σὺν Ἑλλάδι ;
sections.translation: So you have no mind to share this trouble with Hellas ?

sections.label: Section 411
sections.original: Ἑλλὰς δὲ σὺν σοὶ κατὰ θεὸν νοσεῖ τινα .
sections.translation: No , Hellas is diseased like you , according to some god ’ s design .

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: σκήπτρῳ νυν αὔχει , σὸν κασίγνητον προδούς .
sections.translation: Go boast of your scepter , after betraying your own brother ! while [*] I will seek some different means and other friends .

sections.label: Section 414b
sections.original: ὦ Πανελλήνων ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: Agamemnon , lord of Hellas !

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: Ἀγάμεμνον , ἥκω παῖδά σοι τὴν σὴν ἄγων ,
sections.translation: I have come and bring you your daughter , whom you call Iphigenia in your home ; and her mother , your wife Clytemnestra , is with her , and the child Orestes , a sight to gladden [*] you after your long absence from your home ;

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὡς μακρὰν ἔτεινον , εὔρυτον παρὰ
sections.translation: but they had been travelling long and far , they are now resting their tender feet at the waters of a fair spring , they and their horses , for we turned these loose in the grassy meadow to browse their fill . But I have come as their forerunner to prepare you for their reception ;

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ἥκω · πέπυσται γὰρ στρατός — ταχεῖα γὰρ
sections.translation: for the army knows already of your daughter ’ s arrival , so quickly did the rumor spread ; and all the people are running together to the sight , that they may see your child ; for Fortune ’ s favorites enjoy world-wide fame and have all eyes fixed on them .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: λέγουσι δʼ · Ὑμέναιός τις ἢ τί πράσσεται ;
sections.translation: Some say : Is it a wedding , or what is happening ? or has king Agamemnon from fond yearning summoned his daughter here ? From others you would have heard : They are presenting the maiden to Artemis , queen of Aulis , previous to marriage ; who can the bridegroom be , that is to lead her home ?

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶα , τἀπὶ τοισίδʼ ἐξάρχου κανᾶ ,
sections.translation: Come , then , begin the rites , that is the next step , by getting the baskets ready ; crown your heads — you too , [*] lord Menelaus ; prepare the wedding hymn ; let flutes sound throughout the tents with noise of dancer ’ s feet ; for this is a happy day , that has come for the maid .

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ἐπῄνεσʼ , ἀλλὰ στεῖχε δωμάτων ἔσω ·
sections.translation: You have my thanks ; now go within ; for the rest it will be well , as Fate proceeds . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 442
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί φῶ δύστηνος ; ἄρξωμαι πόθεν ;
sections.translation: Ah , woe is me ! unhappy wretch , what can I say ? where shall I begin ? To what cruel straits have I been plunged ! A god has outwitted me , proving far cleverer

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: πολλῷ γενέσθαι τῶν ἐμῶν σοφώτερος .
sections.translation: than any cunning of mine . What an advantage humble birth possesses ! for it is easy for her sons to weep and tell out all their sorrows ; while to the high-born man come these same sorrows , but we heve dignity

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: τὸν ὄγκον ἔχομεν τῷ τʼ ὄχλῳ δουλεύομεν .
sections.translation: throned over our life and are the people ’ s slaves . [*] I , for instance , am ashamed to weep , and no less ashamed , poor wretch , to check my tears at the dreadful pass to which I am brought . Enough ; what am I to tell my wife ?

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: πῶς δέξομαί νιν ; ποῖον ὄμμα συμβαλῶ ;
sections.translation: how shall I welcome her ? with what face meet her ? for she too has undone me by coming uninvited in this my hour of sorrow ; yet it was only natural she should come with her daughter to prepare the bride and perform the fondest duties , where she will discover my villainy .

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: τὴν δʼ αὖ τάλαιναν παρθένον — τί παρθένον ;
sections.translation: And for this poor maid — why maid ? Death , it seems , will soon make her his bride — how I pity her ! Thus will she plead to me , I think : My father , will you slay me ? May you yourself make such a marriage , and whoever is a friend to you !

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: παρὼν δʼ Ὀρέστης ἐγγὺς ἀναβοήσεται
sections.translation: While Orestes , from his station near us , will cry in childish accents , inarticulate , yet fraught with meaning . Alas ! to what utter ruin Paris , the son of Priam , the cause of these troubles , has brought me by his union with Helen !

sections.label: Section 469
sections.original: κἀγὼ κατῴκτιρʼ , ὡς γυναῖκα δεῖ ξένην
sections.translation: I pity her myself , as a woman who is a stranger

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: ὑπὲρ τυράννων συμφορᾶς καταστένειν .
sections.translation: may grieve for the misfortunes of royalty .

sections.label: Section 471
sections.original: ἀδελφέ , δός μοι δεξιᾶς τῆς σῆς θιγεῖν .
sections.translation: Your hand , brother ! let me grasp it .

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: δίδωμι · σὸν γὰρ τὸ κράτος , ἄθλιος δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I give it ; yours is the victory , mine the sorrow .

sections.label: Section 473
sections.original: Πέλοπα κατόμνυμʼ , ὃς πατὴρ τοὐμοῦ πατρὸς
sections.translation: By Pelops our reputed grandsire and Atreus our father ,

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: ἦ μὴν ἐρεῖν σοι τἀπὸ καρδίας σαφῶς
sections.translation: I swear to tell you the truth from my heart , without any covert purpose , but only what I think . The sight of you in tears made me pity you , and in return I shed a tear for you myself ; I withdraw from my former proposals ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: οὐκ ἐς σὲ δεινός · εἰμὶ δʼ οὗπερ εἶ σὺ νῦν ·
sections.translation: ceasing to be a cause of fear to you ; yes , and I will put myself in your present position ; [*] and I counsel you , do not slay your child or prefer my interests to yours ; for it is not just that you should grieve , while I am glad , or that your children should die , while mine still see the light of day .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: τί βούλομαι γάρ ; οὐ γάμους ἐξαιρέτους
sections.translation: What is it , after all , I seek ? If I am set on marriage , could I not find a bride as choice elsewhere ? Was I to lose a brother — the last I should have lost — to win a Helen , getting bad for good ? I was mad , impetuous as a youth , till I perceived ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: σκοπῶν ἐσεῖδον οἷον ἦν κτείνειν τέκνα .
sections.translation: on closer view , what slaying children really meant . Moreover I am filled with compassion for the hapless maiden , doomed to bleed that I may wed , when I reflect that we are kin . What has your daughter to do with Helen ?

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ἴτω στρατεία διαλυθεῖσʼ ἐξ Αὐλίδος ,
sections.translation: Let the army be disbanded and leave Aulis ; dry those streaming eyes , brother , and do not provoke me to tears . Whatever concern you have in oracles that affect your child , let it be none of mine ; into your hands I resign my share .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐς μεταβολὰς ἦλθον ἀπὸ δεινῶν λόγων ;
sections.translation: A sudden change , you ’ ll say , from my dread proposals ? A natural course for me ; affection for my brother caused the change . These are the ways of a man not devoid of virtue , to pursue on each occasion what is best .

sections.label: Section 504
sections.original: γενναῖʼ ἔλεξας Ταντάλῳ τε τῷ Διὸς
sections.translation: A generous speech , worthy of Tantalus , the son of Zeus ;

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: πρέποντα · προγόνους οὐ καταισχύνεις σέθεν .
sections.translation: you do not shame your ancestry .

sections.label: Section 506
sections.original: αἰνῶ σε , Μενέλαʼ , ὅτι παρὰ γνώμην ἐμὴν
sections.translation: I thank you , Menelaus , for this unexpected suggestion ; it is an honorable proposal , worthy of you . Sometimes love , sometimes the selfishness of their families , causes a quarrel between brothers ; I loathe

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: τοιάνδε συγγένειαν ἀλλήλοιν πικράν .
sections.translation: a relationship of this kind which is bitterness to both . [*] But it is useless , for circumstances compel me to carry out the murderous sacrifice of my daughter .
